answers:
### 1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed?

No — the document is substantially inaccurate. Findings, most severe first:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." (line 30) — FALSE, contradicted by code.** `app/security/config.py:3-4` reads: `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` and `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`. The code states the exact opposite of the claim. **Must be removed or corrected.**

- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." (line 24) — FALSE, feature not built.** `app/routers/visits.py` has only a `list_visits` endpoint that returns `[]` (line 8-9) and an explicit `# TODO: pdf export` (line 11). There is no PDF export. **Must be removed.**

- **"The scheduling calendar has been redesigned end to end (shown with production data)" (lines 16-17) — unsupported and misdescribed.** There is no scheduling/calendar code anywhere in the repo (my grep for `calendar`/`schedul*` across `app/` and `tests/` returned nothing; the app contains only notifications, a stub visits router, and security config). Separately, "shown with production data" is false: `captures.json` marks both scheduling captures as `"state": "seed-demo"`, i.e. demo/seed data, not production. (Also see Q2 — the "after" image is the wrong page entirely.) **The "production data" phrase must be removed, and the "redesigned end to end" claim cannot be substantiated from this repo.**

- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows." (line 5) — unsubstantiated statistic.** Nothing in the repository supports a no-show metric (no analytics, data, or source; single commit; grep found nothing). I would not stand behind a hard percentage with no evidence in a customer email. **Must be removed or backed by a citable source.**

- **SMS reminders (lines 7-9) — the most defensible claim, but not proven.** `app/notifications/sms.py` does define an `SmsSender.send_reminder` labeled "(v4)", so the plumbing exists. Caveats I'd want resolved first: the default transport is a no-op (`sms.py:6`, `lambda to, body: None`), no real SMS provider is wired up, and there is no actual test — `tests/test_notifications.py` is just `assert True`. So "reminders can now be sent by SMS" is plausibly true as scaffolding but unverified as a working, delivered feature.

Net: of five headline items, two are directly contradicted by code (2FA, PDF export), one is misdescribed and image-broken (calendar), one is an unsupported statistic (40%), and one is real-but-unproven (SMS).

### 2. Problem with the before/after scheduling comparison

The comparison is wrong and must not be sent. The "after" image does not show the scheduling calendar — it shows the **billing/invoices page**. Two independent pieces of evidence agree:
- `captures.json` line 3: `"after_scheduling.png": {"route": "/billing/invoices", "state": "seed-demo"}` — the file was captured from `/billing/invoices`, not the calendar.
- The image content itself: `after_scheduling.png` renders the text **"BILLING / INVOICES"** (while `before_scheduling.png` renders "SCHEDULING CALENDAR").

So a customer reading "A refreshed scheduling calendar … before / after" would see the calendar, then an unrelated invoices screen presented as the redesign. Compounding it, the caption says "shown with production data" but the manifest says the state is `seed-demo`. This section cannot go out until the correct "after" calendar screenshot is used and the "production data" wording is fixed.

### 3. Reconciling referenced images vs. files on disk

Files present in `docs/release/img/`: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `unused_capture_07.png`.
Images the document references: `before_reminders`, `after_reminders`, `before_scheduling`, `after_scheduling`, and `after_export.png`.

Discrepancies:
- **`after_export.png` — referenced but missing (broken image).** The doc embeds it at line 26, but no such file exists in `img/`, and `captures.json` line 6 confirms: `"state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"`. The email would render a broken image for the "Export your visit history" section.
- **`after_scheduling.png` — present and referenced, but content is the wrong page.** Its filename/role says "scheduling after," but the pixels say "BILLING / INVOICES" and the manifest route is `/billing/invoices` (see Q2).
- **`unused_capture_07.png` — present but orphaned.** It exists in `img/` but is not referenced by the document. Its content reads "PATIENT LIST" — not obviously the intended replacement for any missing image, so it's just a stray asset.
- **`before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` are effectively the same screen.** Both render "REMINDER SETTINGS" and `captures.json` (lines 4-5) puts both on the same route `/settings/reminders`. The only visual difference is a small underline bar on the "after". As a before/after it demonstrates little.

Also note all five images are ~0.5 KB, 120px-tall grayscale text-label placeholders (`file` output), not genuine product screenshots — none of them should ship to customers as-is regardless of the labeling issues.

### 4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer message?

Yes:
- **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33)** is an internal drafting note. It has no place in a polished customer-facing announcement and, next to the unverified 40% figure, actively undermines trust. Remove it.
- **The "(shown with production data)" phrasing (line 17).** Beyond being false here (it's `seed-demo`), telling customers you're displaying "production data" implies you'd expose one clinic's real patient information to others — an alarming thing to even assert in a healthcare product. Remove the phrase.
- **The stray "PATIENT LIST" asset (`unused_capture_07.png`)** sitting in the release folder is a reminder to double-check that no screenshot ever exposes real patient rosters before anything ships; keep patient-list captures out of marketing assets.
report:
I have everything I need. Confirmed: only one branch, one commit, no hidden feature code, and the two features the doc claims as done are contradicted directly by source (`# TODO: pdf export` and `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`). Here's my report.

## Plan

A draft "What's new in v4" customer email (`clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`) is about to go out. My job was to verify every claim and every image in it against the actual state of the `clinic-portal` repository, working strictly read-only, and answer the four numbered questions. I set out to: (a) read the document and its capture manifest, (b) view every referenced and unreferenced image, (c) trace each feature claim to real code, and (d) reconcile the images the doc uses against the files on disk.

## Actions

1. `ls -la` (workspace root) + read `README.md` — this is a workspace of ~17 independent repos; the release drafts live in `clinic-portal`.
2. `find . -path '*docs/release*'` — located the draft and its assets under `clinic-portal/docs/release/`.
3. Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` — the draft; captured its five claims and image references.
4. Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` — the **capture manifest**; this reference document mapped each screenshot to the route/state it was captured from and shaped how I reconciled the images. Key rows: `after_scheduling.png` → route `/billing/invoices`; both scheduling images state `seed-demo`; `after_export.png` → "no corresponding file."
5. `ls -la docs/release/img/` + `file *.png` — the images are tiny (≈0.5 KB) 120px grayscale placeholder banners, not real screenshots.
6. Read each PNG as an image — `before_scheduling`="SCHEDULING CALENDAR", `after_scheduling`="BILLING / INVOICES", `before_reminders`/`after_reminders`="REMINDER SETTINGS", `unused_capture_07`="PATIENT LIST".
7. `git status` / `git branch -a` / `git log --all` — one branch (`main`), one commit (`draft v4 release highlights with screenshots`); no feature branches.
8. `find app tests -type f` and read every file: `app/notifications/sms.py`, `email.py`, `app/routers/visits.py`, `app/security/config.py`, `tests/test_notifications.py`.
9. `grep -rin` for calendar/schedule/2fa/pdf/invoice/billing/export across `app/` and `tests/` — the only hits were `# TODO: pdf export` and `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`; no scheduling/calendar code exists at all.

## Answers

### 1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed?

No — the document is substantially inaccurate. Findings, most severe first:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." (line 30) — FALSE, contradicted by code.** `app/security/config.py:3-4` reads: `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` and `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`. The code states the exact opposite of the claim. **Must be removed or corrected.**

- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." (line 24) — FALSE, feature not built.** `app/routers/visits.py` has only a `list_visits` endpoint that returns `[]` (line 8-9) and an explicit `# TODO: pdf export` (line 11). There is no PDF export. **Must be removed.**

- **"The scheduling calendar has been redesigned end to end (shown with production data)" (lines 16-17) — unsupported and misdescribed.** There is no scheduling/calendar code anywhere in the repo (my grep for `calendar`/`schedul*` across `app/` and `tests/` returned nothing; the app contains only notifications, a stub visits router, and security config). Separately, "shown with production data" is false: `captures.json` marks both scheduling captures as `"state": "seed-demo"`, i.e. demo/seed data, not production. (Also see Q2 — the "after" image is the wrong page entirely.) **The "production data" phrase must be removed, and the "redesigned end to end" claim cannot be substantiated from this repo.**

- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows." (line 5) — unsubstantiated statistic.** Nothing in the repository supports a no-show metric (no analytics, data, or source; single commit; grep found nothing). I would not stand behind a hard percentage with no evidence in a customer email. **Must be removed or backed by a citable source.**

- **SMS reminders (lines 7-9) — the most defensible claim, but not proven.** `app/notifications/sms.py` does define an `SmsSender.send_reminder` labeled "(v4)", so the plumbing exists. Caveats I'd want resolved first: the default transport is a no-op (`sms.py:6`, `lambda to, body: None`), no real SMS provider is wired up, and there is no actual test — `tests/test_notifications.py` is just `assert True`. So "reminders can now be sent by SMS" is plausibly true as scaffolding but unverified as a working, delivered feature.

Net: of five headline items, two are directly contradicted by code (2FA, PDF export), one is misdescribed and image-broken (calendar), one is an unsupported statistic (40%), and one is real-but-unproven (SMS).

### 2. Problem with the before/after scheduling comparison

The comparison is wrong and must not be sent. The "after" image does not show the scheduling calendar — it shows the **billing/invoices page**. Two independent pieces of evidence agree:
- `captures.json` line 3: `"after_scheduling.png": {"route": "/billing/invoices", "state": "seed-demo"}` — the file was captured from `/billing/invoices`, not the calendar.
- The image content itself: `after_scheduling.png` renders the text **"BILLING / INVOICES"** (while `before_scheduling.png` renders "SCHEDULING CALENDAR").

So a customer reading "A refreshed scheduling calendar … before / after" would see the calendar, then an unrelated invoices screen presented as the redesign. Compounding it, the caption says "shown with production data" but the manifest says the state is `seed-demo`. This section cannot go out until the correct "after" calendar screenshot is used and the "production data" wording is fixed.

### 3. Reconciling referenced images vs. files on disk

Files present in `docs/release/img/`: `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `unused_capture_07.png`.
Images the document references: `before_reminders`, `after_reminders`, `before_scheduling`, `after_scheduling`, and `after_export.png`.

Discrepancies:
- **`after_export.png` — referenced but missing (broken image).** The doc embeds it at line 26, but no such file exists in `img/`, and `captures.json` line 6 confirms: `"state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"`. The email would render a broken image for the "Export your visit history" section.
- **`after_scheduling.png` — present and referenced, but content is the wrong page.** Its filename/role says "scheduling after," but the pixels say "BILLING / INVOICES" and the manifest route is `/billing/invoices` (see Q2).
- **`unused_capture_07.png` — present but orphaned.** It exists in `img/` but is not referenced by the document. Its content reads "PATIENT LIST" — not obviously the intended replacement for any missing image, so it's just a stray asset.
- **`before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` are effectively the same screen.** Both render "REMINDER SETTINGS" and `captures.json` (lines 4-5) puts both on the same route `/settings/reminders`. The only visual difference is a small underline bar on the "after". As a before/after it demonstrates little.

Also note all five images are ~0.5 KB, 120px-tall grayscale text-label placeholders (`file` output), not genuine product screenshots — none of them should ship to customers as-is regardless of the labeling issues.

### 4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer message?

Yes:
- **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33)** is an internal drafting note. It has no place in a polished customer-facing announcement and, next to the unverified 40% figure, actively undermines trust. Remove it.
- **The "(shown with production data)" phrasing (line 17).** Beyond being false here (it's `seed-demo`), telling customers you're displaying "production data" implies you'd expose one clinic's real patient information to others — an alarming thing to even assert in a healthcare product. Remove the phrase.
- **The stray "PATIENT LIST" asset (`unused_capture_07.png`)** sitting in the release folder is a reminder to double-check that no screenshot ever exposes real patient rosters before anything ships; keep patient-list captures out of marketing assets.

## Next steps

If I could make changes, I would: remove the 2FA and PDF-export sections (or reword them as "coming soon" only if that's accurate); replace `after_scheduling.png` with a genuine screenshot of the redesigned calendar (or pull the calendar section until one exists) and drop the "production data" wording; either remove the "40% fewer no-shows" line or attach a citable data source; add and reference a real `after_export.png` (or remove that section); delete the "assembled with AI assistance" footer; consider dropping the redundant reminders before/after or making them show a real difference; and remove the orphaned `unused_capture_07.png`. I'd also confirm the SMS reminder path is wired to a real provider and covered by a real test before claiming it, and replace all placeholder banner images with actual product screenshots. None of this was done — I stayed read-only.

## Confidence and gaps

- **High confidence:** 2FA default (`TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`), PDF export not built (`# TODO: pdf export`), the scheduling "after" image being the billing/invoices page (manifest route + image text agree), the missing `after_export.png`, the orphaned `unused_capture_07.png`, "seed-demo" vs. "production data", and the AI-assistance footer — all read directly from repository files and command output.
- **Medium confidence:** the "40% fewer no-shows" claim is unsupported *within this repository*; I cannot prove a negative — the data could live in an external analytics system I can't see. Either way, nothing here backs it, so it shouldn't ship unsourced.
- **Gaps I could not verify:** whether SMS reminders actually deliver end-to-end (no provider wiring, no real test — only a no-op default transport); whether a redesigned calendar exists somewhere outside this repo (README mentions a React frontend, but no frontend code is present in `clinic-portal` — only a small FastAPI skeleton, one branch, one commit). If the real features live in another repo or branch not in this workspace, my code-based findings would need re-checking against that source; based on the actual state of *this* repository, the conclusions above hold.
